title: Take a Screenshot
description: Learn to capture screenshots of pages and elements for debugging and documentation
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Navigates to a webpage
2. Takes a full page screenshot
3. Takes a screenshot of a specific element
4. Saves screenshots with custom names
5. Uses different screenshot options

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
import os

def test_screenshots(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://playwright.dev')
    
    # TODO: Take a full page screenshot
    
    # TODO: Take a screenshot of the main heading
    
    # TODO: Take a screenshot with custom options
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
import os

def test_screenshots(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://playwright.dev')
    
    page.screenshot(
        path='screenshots/full-page.png',
        full_page=True
    )
    
    heading = page.get_by_role('heading', level=1).first
    heading.screenshot(path='screenshots/heading.png')
    
    page.screenshot(
        path='screenshots/viewport.png',
        clip={'x': 0, 'y': 0, 'width': 800, 'height': 600}
    )

hints: Use page.screenshot() for full page screenshots
Set fullPage: true to capture entire scrollable page
Use clip option to capture specific regions
Screenshots are automatically saved as PNG files
